settings.title: EmDash CMS
settings.tagline: An open source Astro CMS built for humans and agents.

content.headline: A CMS for humans and agents
content.subheadline: EmDash is an open source CMS for Astro and Cloudflare. Define content in code, edit it in a polished admin, and let agents work with it through the same APIs you do.

content.headline: Built for the way you actually ship
content.subheadline: Type-safe content, server-rendered Astro pages, and a single binary deploy on Cloudflare. No SaaS dashboard between you and your data.

features.title: Schema as code
features.description: Define collections, fields, and relationships in a seed file. Get generated TypeScript types and a fully migrated database every time you run dev.

features.title: Astro-native
features.description: First-class Astro integration. Server-rendered pages, content loaders, image components, and Portable Text rendering with no glue code.

features.title: Runs on Cloudflare
features.description: D1 for content, R2 for media, Workers for serving. One stack, global by default, deployed from a single command.

features.title: Admin humans love
features.description: Drafts, revisions, scheduling, full-text search, taxonomies, menus, and live preview, all in a fast modern editor.

features.title: Built for agents
features.description: Every CMS operation is exposed over MCP. Agents read schema, edit content, and publish through the same typed APIs the admin uses.

features.title: Extensible by design
features.description: Plugins add custom block types, admin pages, hooks, and storage. Bring your own auth, payments, comments, or analytics.

testimonials.quote: The entire functionality of WordPress, and then some.
testimonials.author: Matt Mullenweg

testimonials.quote: The most interesting thing to happen to content management in years.
testimonials.author: Joost de Valk

testimonials.quote: Getting from zero to a basic design is fast. I mean, really fast.
testimonials.author: Brian Coords

items.question: What is EmDash?
items.answer: EmDash is an open source CMS built on Astro and Cloudflare. It gives you a typed schema, a polished admin, and an MCP server so both humans and AI agents can manage your content.

items.question: How is it different from other CMSes?
items.answer: EmDash is Astro-native and self-hosted on your own Cloudflare account. Your schema lives in code, your data lives in D1 and R2, and there is no SaaS backend between you and your content.

items.question: Can AI agents really edit content?
items.answer: Yes. EmDash ships a Model Context Protocol server that exposes content, schema, media, taxonomies, menus, and revisions. Drop it into Claude, OpenCode, or any MCP-aware tool and it just works.

items.question: How do I get started?
items.answer: Open the playground for an instant sandbox, or run npx emdash dev in a fresh Astro project to spin up a local instance with the admin at /_emdash/admin.

items.question: Where can I ask questions?
items.answer: Join the Discord to chat with the team and community, or open an issue on GitHub. The docs cover setup, architecture, and the full API reference.

data.title: EmDash is live
data.excerpt: A quick look at what EmDash is, what shipped in the first public release, and where to go next.

children.text: EmDash is an open source CMS built on Astro. It combines a full admin UI, structured content, and a real site codebase without splitting editing and development into separate systems.
This site will be used for release announcements, product updates, and major changes to the project. If you want to explore EmDash right now, head to the playground or read through the docs.
